format.environment: Your IDE (IntelliJ, VS Code), Google Meet screen share

tips: Clarify functional requirements before implementing — a few minutes is enough
There are multiple stages — keep each stage simple so you can complete more
Code quality and testing matter heavily
Refresh concurrency knowledge — they want high-performance solutions

problems.name: Load Balancer

leetcode_practice.why: TTL pattern → auto-deregister on heartbeat expiry

requirements: register(server) — register a backend server (max 10, unique addresses)
deregister(server) — remove a server from the pool
get() — return a server using Random strategy
expectations: Strategy Pattern — pluggable algorithms via interface
Thread Safety — Lock, ReentrantReadWriteLock, or ConcurrentHashMap
Proper Error Handling — throw exceptions, not return strings
Tests — happy path + edge cases + concurrency
SOLID Principles — open for extension, closed for modification

problems.name: URL Shortener

leetcode_practice.why: Capacity + eviction policy if MAX_URLS overflows

requirements: shorten(url) — return a short URL (4-6 alphanumeric chars)
Duplicate full URLs should return the existing short URL

problems.name: Money Transfer / Account Ledger
problems.key_insight: Consistent lock ordering to prevent deadlock — sort accounts by ID before acquiring locks

common_mistakes: Returning strings for errors — throw custom exceptions instead
Missing get() method on Load Balancer
Tests only for happy path — include edge cases
